text.title: azure-private-link Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-private-link as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Private Endpoints break public DNS by default in the same VNet. Without the `privatelink.*` zone linked to every consumer VNet, resolution lands on the public IP and connects fine — to the public endpoint, defeating the entire purpose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Azure DNS resolver checks for Private DNS zone overrides. If no zone is linked, the standard CNAME chain resolves to the public IP. There is no error — the connection succeeds, but it goes over the internet instead of the Azure backbone.

failure-mode.description: Service Endpoints and Private Endpoints look interchangeable but are not: Service Endpoints leak via subnet identity; Private Endpoints inject an IP into your VNet.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Service Endpoints allow subnet-based access to PaaS services but the service still has a public IP. Private Endpoints make the service genuinely private — no public IP exposure.

failure-mode.description: Private Endpoints in module or Bicep loops without unique `name` collide silently — last-write-wins.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When creating multiple Private Endpoints via `for_each` or `count`, each must have a unique name. Duplicate names cause silent overwrites in Terraform state or ARM deployment failures.
